ideation: This sequence will depict a bird in flight.  The arms will mimic wings, with a flapping motion. Because the robot has no elbows, the animation will focus on shoulder movement to simulate the up-and-down motion of wings. Frame 1 will show both arms extended to the sides, slightly downward, representing the starting position of the wing flap. Frame 2 will have the arms raised and angled slightly back, simulating the upward stroke of the wings. Frame 3 will return the arms to a position similar to Frame 1, completing the downstroke. Frame 4 will have the arms raised again, but slightly less than in Frame 2, to show a less powerful second upstroke. Frame 5 will return the arms to a position of rest, slightly lower than Frame 1, and closer to the body.

frames.beat: arms extend out and down, ready to fly
wings push down, completing the stroke
a second, shorter upward flap of the wings
arms settle closer to the body, resting
